channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Autocratic regimes 'relishing' USAID gutting: Samantha Power
item.description: Samantha Power, a former administrator of the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID), says autocratic regimes across the world are “relishing” the Trump administration's efforts to gut the agency. “It's not even an opinion.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: International Criminal Court condemns Trump-imposed sanctions
item.description: The International Criminal Court (ICC) on Friday condemned President Trump’s executive order imposing sanctions on the global tribunal. Trump ordered the sanctions in response to the ICC issuing arrest warrants for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Israel's former defense chief.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: DOJ disbands foreign influence task force, unit tasked with seizing Russian oligarchs' asset
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi disbanded a Biden-era initiative targeting Russian oligarchs as well as another designed to combat foreign influence.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Russia claims it's taken another eastern Ukraine town. Kyiv officials make no comment
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia’s Defense Ministry claimed on Friday that its forces had captured the coal mining town of Toretsk in their latest breakthrough in eastern Ukraine’s Donetsk region, where Ukrainian defenses are creaking.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and BARRY HATTON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Forced leaves start for thousands at USAID under a Trump plan to gut the foreign aid agency
item.description: Forced leaves have begun in Washington and worldwide for most employees of the U.S. Agency for International Development.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ukraine's Western backers will meet for arms talks as doubts over US intentions grow
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — The main international forum for drumming up weapons and ammunition for Ukraine will for the first time meet under the auspices of a country other than the U.S. as uncertainty surrounds the future of Washington’s support for arming the war-torn country.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Arab, Muslim Americans blast Trump's 'grotesque' Gaza proposal
item.description: Arab American and Muslim American leaders are blasting President Trump’s proposal to take over Gaza, warning that it’s antithetical to their beliefs and amounts to ethnic cleansing.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, The Hill

item.title: Turkey ends Afghan diplomats' mission, paving way for Taliban to appoint their own team
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — Turkey has terminated the mission of Afghan diplomats who were appointed by Afghanistan’s former, pro-Western government, the outgoing diplomatic team said, in a move that paves the way for the Taliban to appoint their own envoys.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: ICC condemns sanctions by Trump, pledges to continue its work
item.description: The International Criminal Court said the move was an attempt to “harm its independent and impartial judicial work.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Iran supreme leader criticizes proposed nuclear talks with US, upending push to negotiation
item.description: Iran’s supreme leader says that negotiations with America “are not intelligent, wise or honorable” after President Donald Trump floated nuclear talks with Tehran.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Kosovo elections will decide who will lead stalled talks with Serbia and manage a battered economy
item.description: PRISTINA, Kosovo (AP) — Kosovo goes to the polls Sunday in a parliamentary election expected to be a key test for Prime Minister Albin Kurti after his governing party won in a landslide four years ago.
item.dc:creator: FLORENT BAJRAMI and LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Frozen US aid brings new challenges to groups hit by harsh crackdowns in Russia and Belarus
item.description: Rights groups, activists and independent media in Russia and Belarus endured increased government scrutiny, repressive laws and even were shut down and forced to operate from exile abroad.
item.dc:creator: Dasha Litvinova And Yuras Karmanau, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump executive order sanctions international court over Israel war crimes case
item.description: President Trump on Thursday issued an executive order sanctioning the International Criminal Court (ICC) over its pursuit of war crimes cases against Israel’s prime minister and its former defense minister.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: From fighting disease to protecting the Amazon rainforest, USAID has big impact across the globe
item.description: The Trump administration’s decision to close the U.S. Agency for International Development has drawn widespread criticism from congressional Democrats and raised questions and concern about the influence billionaire ally Elon Musk wields over the federal government.
item.dc:creator: KIMBERLEE KRUESI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jeffries amps up call for two-state solution after Trump push to seize Gaza
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) on Wednesday flatly rejected President Trump’s proposal for the United States to take over the Gaza Strip, suggesting it would undermine ongoing efforts to free Israeli hostages and bring peace to the volatile region.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, WGN 9

item.title: USPS resumes accepting packages from China, Hong Kong
item.description: USPS says it's working with CBP to implement a collection process for new China tariffs to avoid delivery disruptions.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Mexico says it will not allow US to send Mexican migrants to Guantanamo Bay
item.description: Mexico told the the U.S. government it will not allow Mexican migrants to be sent to the Guantánamo Bay, Cuba, detention camp, the country's foreign minister said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Saudi Arabia says it won't establish ties with Israel without creation of Palestinian state
item.description: Saudi Arabia said on Wednesday it would not establish ties with Israel without the creation of a Palestinian state.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's idea to clear Gaza is a real estate grab: Rep. Auchincloss
item.description: President Trump and his son-in-law view the Gaza Strip as “developable property," a Democratic lawmaker says.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Trump floats US taking over Gaza Strip
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday floated the idea of the U.S. taking over the Gaza Strip, saying America should be responsible for clearing the territory of rubble and unexploded bombs and proposing an “economic development.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: Trump warns he left instructions to ‘obliterate’ Iran if he is assassinated
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday said that he has left instructions that if Iran were to assassination him, the country should be destroyed.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Uganda starts clinical trial of vaccine for Sudan strain of Ebola amid new outbreak
item.description: Uganda has begun a clinical trial of a vaccine against the Sudan strain of Ebola following an outbreak that has killed one nurse in the east African country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: PM declares 'worst mass shooting in Swedish history' after gunman kills 10 on school campus
item.description: Swedish police are responding to a school shooting in Orebro, Sweden, on Tuesday after reports said at least 10 people were killed
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump wants rare earth elements from Ukraine in exchange for military aid
item.description: “We're looking to do a deal with Ukraine, where they're going to secure what we're giving them with their rare earth and other things,” he told reporters.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WGN 9

item.title: El Salvador agrees to accept US deportees of any nationality following meeting with Rubio
item.description: El Salvador's President Nayib Bukele has offered to accept convicted criminals of any nationality the U.S. wants to deport and house them in his country's prison system.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EU warns Trump it would ‘respond firmly’ to tariffs
item.description: “The EU would respond firmly to any trading partner that unfairly or arbitrarily imposes tariffs on EU goods,” a spokesperson said. “There is a lot at stake.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Venezuela frees 6 Americans after meeting between President Maduro and Trump's envoy
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Six Americans who had been detained in Venezuela in recent months were freed by the government of President Nicolás Maduro after he met Friday with a Trump administration official tasked with urging the authoritarian leader to take back deported migrants who have committed
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO and JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation
